name: ComfyUI Renderer Adapter
purpose: Turn keyframe and repair job JSON files into controlled ComfyUI batch payloads for RunPod or local ComfyUI.
why_comfyui: The project already uses ComfyUI-style generation thinking: prompts, negative prompts, output targets and review loops.
RunPod ComfyUI can generate the heavy images while the dashboard stays lightweight.
A thin adapter lets us swap workflows without rewriting the whole production system.
Batch payloads make it easier for an agent to process Shot 006, Shot 009 and queued shots in order.
render_rules: Never put dialogue or readable text inside the generated image.
Repair jobs have priority over fresh queued shots.
Shot 006 must avoid front-facing paper and infographic layouts.
Every output must go back through TV Review before assembly.
ComfyUI should be treated as a renderer, not as the production brain.
first_batch_goal: Generate a clean prioritized image batch plan for Shot 006, Shot 009 and queued shots 007, 008, 010 and 011.
